steps: 1. The cost of one book is £n. 2. Sam buys 2 books. 3. Total cost = cost per book × number of books = n × 2 = 2n. 4. Check options: A) n + 2 is addition, not multiplication by 2. B) 2n matches n × 2. C) n - 2 is subtraction. D) n × n is n squared, not multiplied by 2. E) n ÷ 2 is half of n, not double. 5. Only 2n correctly represents the total cost.
